# UC-17.1.59 · Cisco ISE Logs through Splunk Edge Processor — Pipeline Health and Routing

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Operations, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*ISE sends information to Splunk through a middleman. We watch the middleman to make sure it isn't dropping or breaking messages on the way through.*

---

## Description

Detects Splunk Edge Processor / Ingest Processor pipeline issues that affect ISE log ingestion — drops, parse errors, routing failures.

## Value

When Edge Processor is in the path between ISE and Splunk indexers, pipeline health is the missing link in observability. A 5% drop rate is invisible from the Splunk indexer side but obvious from EP metrics.

## Implementation

Confirm Edge Processor is sending self-telemetry to Splunk. Schedule every 15 min, search window `-30m@m to @m`.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Misconfigure an Edge Processor pipeline regex causing 5% parse errors. UC-17.1.59 fires for that pipeline.

### Negative scenario

Fix the regex. Drops return to 0. Alert clears.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Edge Processor or Ingest Processor deployed in path.
- Self-telemetry enabled.

### Step 1 — Verify telemetry feed.
### Step 2 — Search.
### Step 3 — Provoke as above.
### Step 4 — Dashboard with drop-rate per pipeline.
### Step 5 — Troubleshooting: tune pipeline regex; verify EP capacity.

## SPL

```spl
index=_internal sourcetype="ep:metrics" earliest=-30m
| stats sum(events_in) as in, sum(events_out) as out, sum(dropped_events) as drops, sum(parse_errors) as parse_errors by pipeline_id
| eval drop_pct = round((drops/in)*100, 2)
| where drop_pct > 1 OR parse_errors > 0
```

## Visualization

(1) Per-pipeline drop-rate timechart. (2) Top-N parse errors. (3) Routing-decision distribution.

## Known False Positives

- **Initial pipeline deployment** — High parse errors during initial pipeline configuration; expected for first hour.

## References

- [Splunk Edge Processor Documentation](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/SplunkCloud/9.2.2406/EdgeProcessor)
